rulings.Case Title: MILTON GODBEE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declaration of Rudy Limpert creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable for plaintiff¿s alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant¿s Ammco brake grinding machine. While such machine itself did not contain asbestos or asbestos-containing component parts, there is a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to plaintiff. Plaintiff¿s objections to the declarations of Craig Mountz and Russell Darnell are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON DUNN et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Benjamin Moore & Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Charles Dunn, Jr.¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar, as it shows that plaintiffs have evidence that decedent performed acts (sanding) that exposed him to Kuhls Seamlast, the only argument made by defendant in its summary judgment motion. However, even if defendant had made asbestos content of such product an issue, Charles Dunn, Jr.¿s deposition testimony, defendant¿s General Order No. 129 interrogatory responses and Mr. Minchew¿s deposition testimony create a triable issue whether decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages did not violate CRC 3.1350 and plaintiffs¿ objections thereto are overruled. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ objection regarding defendant attaching one verification to plaintiffs¿ special interrogatory responses is overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Charles Dunn, Jr.¿s deposition testimony are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
